summary: Verified the database-per-tenant → collection-prefix-per-tenant architecture switch. New pytest suite /app/backend/tests/test_multitenant_prefix.py (18 tests, all PASS) covers: master session/list/rollup, Default society regression (flats/users/invoices/complaints/expenses/staff/invoice-stats — all non-empty and penalty-enriched), society lifecycle (POST /api/master/societies writes prefixed collections `s_<hex>__*` in the shared `maintyn_db` and does NOT create any new physical DB; new admin can log in; new-society flat is invisible to Default society), delete lifecycle (DELETE /api/master/societies/{id} drops all `s_<hex>__*` collections + user_index + society doc, Default remains intact with 33 flats and 175 invoices), and layout sanity (shared DB has societies/master_users/user_index + tenant `s_<hex>__*` collections; exactly 1 is_default society). Direct-mongo check confirmed the six pre-existing legacy `maintyn_society_<uuid>` physical DBs are dormant leftovers; new writes only touch `maintyn_db`. Broader regression: after fixing two test fixtures that referenced legacy paths (test_penalty.py + test_m416_followup.py), the entire suite is 145 passed / 2 failed (147 tests). Remaining 2 failures are pre-existing and unrelated to this task: (1) TestPasswordReset::test_reset_password_valid_then_login_then_restore, (2) test_master_multitenant.py::test_master_auth_me_401_ignored (test expects /api/auth/me to return 401 for master token, but it now returns 200 with master identity — behavior change in auth, not DB layout).

minor.issue: Returns 200 with master identity when called with a master token (test_master_multitenant.py expects 401/403). Unrelated to this iteration's DB migration but should be triaged — either the endpoint should reject master tokens or the test expectation should be updated.
test_reset_password_valid_then_login_then_restore in backend_test.py fails (pre-existing). Not investigated deeply — flag for future iteration.

action_items: Consider deleting the six legacy physical DBs (maintyn_master, maintyn_society_*) from preview mongo — they are dead data (~24MB) and only kept for rollback safety. There is also one stray physical DB `s_a23b56b9ec1e472e9b9817d54933de6e` (empty/near-empty) from an earlier attempt — safe to drop.

Top-level un-prefixed collections still exist in maintyn_db (flats, invoices, users, complaints, expenses, society, staff, etc.) from the migration snapshot. They are not being written to by the new prefixed code (verified: default prefix invoices=175 vs top-level invoices=8), but they consume ~space and could confuse a future engineer. Recommend a cleanup migration.
Decide whether /api/auth/me should 401 for master tokens (as the older test_master_multitenant test expects) or officially return master identity — then align the test.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py _drop_society_data iterates list_collection_names() and drops any starting with the prefix — correct and safe. Consider also removing the society doc + user_index inside the same function for atomicity (currently done in the caller).
list_societies loops N societies × 3 count queries each — for 100+ societies this becomes 300+ round-trips. Consider a single aggregation.

create_society calls sdb.<coll>.create_index for 14 collections synchronously — for a create-society burst this is ~14 index-create RPCs. Acceptable for now, could be parallelised with asyncio.gather.
_migrate_legacy_databases_if_present uses a `master_settings.key='legacy_migration'` sentinel — good for idempotency. Confirmed by direct mongo that this ran once and won't re-run.

Top-level un-prefixed collections in maintyn_db (flats, invoices, users, complaints, expenses, society, staff, utility_bills, utility_connections, visitors, files, announcements, amenities, bookings, digest_runs, password_reset_tokens) appear to be residue from a migration or an earlier code path. Verify nothing writes there and add a one-shot cleanup.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_penalty.py (fixed default_society_db fixture to use shared DB + collection prefix; was pointing at legacy maintyn_society_<hex> physical DB)
/app/backend/tests/test_m416_followup.py (updated backdate-invoice mongo scan to look at prefixed collections in shared DB before falling back to legacy physical DBs)

success_rate.backend: 98.6% (145/147 including regression; 18/18 for the new architecture-specific tests)

test_credentials: admin@maintyn.app / Admin@12345 ; master@maintyn.in / Master@12345 ; demo.resident@example.com / Resident@123
seed_data_creation: During test run: 1 temporary society (name TEST_PrefixSoc_<tag>) + 1 flat inside it were created and then DELETED as part of the lifecycle test. A cleanup fixture (test_zzz_cleanup) sweeps any leftover TEST_* societies + prefixed collections. Final DB state: 1 default society, no test residue.
context_for_next_testing_agent: New authoritative regression file for the multi-tenant architecture change is /app/backend/tests/test_multitenant_prefix.py. Run with: `pytest backend/tests/test_multitenant_prefix.py -o 'addopts=' -v`. It reads MONGO_URL/DB_NAME from /app/backend/.env and REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env, hits the preview URL for API and localhost mongo directly for layout inspection. Tenant collection naming convention is `s_<society_id_no_dashes>__<coll>` in the shared `maintyn_db`. Legacy physical DBs (maintyn_master, maintyn_society_*) are dormant — do not write to them. Two pre-existing failing tests (password-reset restore + master-token /api/auth/me) are known and NOT caused by this iteration.
